name: Suspicious Number of Modifications Made on Virtual Machines
notes:
This rule triggers when a suspicious number of configuration updates are performed on virtual machines. A typical administration rarely implies more than 10 configuration updates, such as adding more memory or reducing the storage size on one or multiple machines in a short period of time.

Note: Populate the reference set "Authorized Users" with users who are authorized to perform massive updates.
conditions_text: when an event matches any of the following BB:DeviceDefinition: Cloud, BB:DeviceDefinition: Virtualization
when an event matches any of the following BB:CategoryDefinition: Virtual Machine Configuration Change, BB:CategoryDefinition: System Configuration
when the event matches Machine Identifier (custom) is not N/A
when any of Username are contained in any of Authorized Users - AlphaNumeric
when at least 10 events are seen with the same Username in 15 minutes
thresholds: when at least 10 events are seen with the same Username in 15 minutes
TD_list: APPLY Suspicious Number of Modifications Made on Virtual Machines on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when an event matches any of the following BB:DeviceDefinition: Cloud, BB:DeviceDefinition: Virtualization
AND when an event matches any of the following BB:CategoryDefinition: Virtual Machine Configuration Change, BB:CategoryDefinition: System Configuration
AND when the event matches Machine Identifier (custom) is not N/A
AND NOT when any of Username are contained in any of Authorized Users - AlphaNumeric
AND when at least 10 events are seen with the same Username in 15 minutes
RED_list: Event name: Suspicious Number of Modifications Made on Virtual Machines
Event description: Detects a suspicious number of configuration updates performed on virtual machines.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Username
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
